steps.title: Identify the singular subject
steps.content: The subject of the verb is "similarity," not the plural "groups." The passive construction needs a form of "be" before "evaluated" that agrees with the singular subject.

steps.title: Reject agreement errors
steps.content: Choice A and Choice C are plural forms, so they do not agree with "similarity." Choice B is singular but past tense, which does not fit the general present-tense description. The remaining option is singular and present tense. The answer is D.
